subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Heat Exhaustion Explained
description: A fast breakdown of Heat Exhaustion Explained for builders.

hook.text: A marathon runner staggers, dizzy and nauseous. Their skin is pale and clammy. This is heat exhaustion.

buildup.text: Heat exhaustion occurs when the body loses too much water and salt through excessive sweating.

discovery.text: Symptoms include heavy sweating, weakness, headache, nausea, and a core temperature up to 104°F.

twist.text: The body is still trying to cool itself—sweating is active. If it stops sweating, it's now heatstroke.

climax.text: Moving to shade, drinking cool water, and removing excess clothing usually reverses heat exhaustion.

punchline.text: Sweating and dizzy? Your body is warning you. Stop, cool down, or it gets worse.

quiz.question: What distinguishes heat exhaustion from heatstroke?
options: In heat exhaustion the body is still sweating and trying to cool down
